analysis_methodology.upstream_analysis: Identified all programs that directly INCLUDE this copybook
analysis_methodology.downstream_analysis: Traced programs called by direct users to identify transitive impact
analysis_methodology.shared_data_analysis: Analyzed which programs share this copybook
analysis_methodology.impact_scoring: Applied scoring matrix based on: direct callers (30%), transitive depth (20%), copybook breadth (25%), complexity (15%), risk flags (10%)

affected_programs.reason: Directly includes CUSTOMER-RECORD copybook; fundamental customer processing logic
key_concerns: Core customer processing logic must be regression tested
Called by BATCH-RUNNER - impacts batch processing

affected_programs.reason: Directly includes CUSTOMER-RECORD copybook; entry point for batch processing
key_concerns: No upstream callers - independent testing possible
Must verify batch processing workflow end-to-end

affected_programs.reason: Directly includes CUSTOMER-RECORD copybook; also uses ACCOUNT-RECORD and PAYMENT-RECORD
key_concerns: MEDIUM complexity - more extensive testing required
Calls PAYMENT-HANDLER - changes cascade to payment processing
Critical business logic for account management

affected_programs.reason: Called by ACCOUNT-MGR which uses CUSTOMER-RECORD; indirect impact through data flow
key_concerns: Contains GOTO statements - high refactoring risk
Does not directly use CUSTOMER-RECORD but receives data from ACCOUNT-MGR
MEDIUM complexity with control flow risks
Regression test for data flow from upstream changes

recommended_change_sequence.validation: End-to-end system test with production-like data volume

recommended_change_sequence.description: Business validation of customer processing changes
recommended_change_sequence.validation: Business users validate customer data processing

risk_assessment.risk_level_justification: CUSTOMER-RECORD is a core copybook used by 3 programs in a critical call chain. Changes will propagate through batch processing, customer management, account management, and payment processing.

critical_risk_factors.description: 3 programs directly include this copybook. Any structural change affects all 3 simultaneously.
Changes propagate through 4-level call chain: BATCH-RUNNER → CUSTOMER-PROC → ACCOUNT-MGR → PAYMENT-HANDLER
ACCOUNT-MGR uses multiple copybooks together. Structural changes may create data mapping issues.
PAYMENT-HANDLER contains GOTO statements, increasing risk of control flow issues if data structures change
Affected programs access 5 different files. Changes to data structure may require file format updates.

mitigation_strategies.description: Test and deploy programs incrementally rather than all at once
Create automated test suite for all 4 affected programs before making changes
If field sizes or types change, plan for data migration of existing files
Freeze all other changes to these 4 programs during the CUSTOMER-RECORD modification
Before making copybook changes, refactor GOTO statements in PAYMENT-HANDLER to reduce control flow risk

estimated_total_effort.note: Assumes 2-person team with parallel work where possible

tree: │   ├── CUSTOMER-PROC (58 lines, LOW complexity, 1 caller, 1 callee)
│   ├── BATCH-RUNNER (124 lines, LOW complexity, 0 callers, 1 callee)
│   └── ACCOUNT-MGR (116 lines, MEDIUM complexity, 1 caller, 1 callee)
└── PAYMENT-HANDLER (119 lines, MEDIUM complexity, has GOTO)
└── Risk: GOTO statements + data flow changes

immediate_actions: Create comprehensive test data covering all scenarios before making any changes
Set up automated regression test suite for all 4 programs
Document current behavior of CUSTOMER-PROC, BATCH-RUNNER, and ACCOUNT-MGR
Freeze all other development on these 4 programs during the change window
pre_change_activities: Review and refactor GOTO statements in PAYMENT-HANDLER
Verify file format compatibility if field sizes/types will change
Create rollback plan including data backups
post_change_activities: Monitor production batch jobs for 1 week after deployment
Review performance metrics - structure changes may impact processing time
Document lessons learned for future copybook changes
architectural_recommendations: Consider creating service layer to reduce direct copybook coupling
Refactor PAYMENT-HANDLER to eliminate GOTO statements
Evaluate splitting CUSTOMER-RECORD if it's frequently changed
Implement automated impact analysis tooling for future changes

compliance_and_audit.change_tracking: Document all modifications to CUSTOMER-RECORD structure in change management system
compliance_and_audit.data_privacy: Verify no PII/sensitive data exposure if field visibility changes
compliance_and_audit.regulatory_impact: If CUSTOMER-RECORD contains regulatory data (e.g., banking, healthcare), ensure compliance team reviews changes
compliance_and_audit.backup_requirements: Full backup of all affected programs and data files before deployment

metadata.analysis_approach: Static graph analysis based on Neo4j knowledge graph data
limitations: Analysis based on static code structure only
